title: Game of the Century
intro: In this game, watch for how each player develops their pieces while controlling key squares near the kings. Pay attention to hanging pieces and the tactical opportunities that arise as the game progresses.
summary: The pressure around both kings intensified as the game progressed. White's pieces were forced into defensive positions, while Black's pieces coordinated effectively to deliver checks. Ultimately, Black's final moves led to a decisive checkmate.

steps.explanation: White plays Nf3, attacking squares d4, e5, g5, and h4.
Black responds with Nf6, attacking squares d5, e4, g4, and h5.
White plays c4, creating a hanging pawn on c4 and attacking squares a4, b5, and d5.
Black plays g6, attacking f5 and removing the hanging rook on h8.
White plays Nc3, attacking e4.
Black plays Bg7, removing the hanging bishop on g7 and attacking h8.
White plays d4, attacking c5, f4, and h6.
Black plays O-O, removing the hanging bishop on g7 and attacking g7.
White plays Bf4, creating a hanging bishop on g7 and attacking a1, c7, and d6.
Black plays d5, attacking c4 and h3.
White plays Qb3, removing the hanging pawns on b2 and c4 while attacking b2, b4, b6, b7, and c4.
Black plays dxc4, capturing the pawn on c4.
White plays Qxc4, capturing the pawn on c4 and attacking a6, c6, e6, and f7.
Black plays c6, attacking a5 and b5.
White plays e4, removing the hanging queen on c4 and attacking c4 and f5.
Black plays Nbd7, attacking c5 and e5.
White plays Rd1, removing the hanging rook on a1.
Black plays Nb6, removing the hanging rook on a8 and attacking a4, a8, c4, d4, and h3.
White plays Qc5, attacking a5, b6, e7, and h5.
Black plays Bg4, creating a hanging pawn on b7 and attacking f3.
White plays Bg5, removing the hanging bishop on f4 and attacking f4 and f6.
Black plays Na4, creating a hanging knight on a4 and attacking a5, b2, c3, and c5. A fork is created threatening b2, c3, and c5.
White plays Qa3, removing the hanging pawn on b2 and attacking b2.
Black plays Nxc3, capturing the pawn on c3 and removing the hanging knight on a4.
White plays bxc3, capturing the knight on c3 and reducing enemy attacks in the king zone.
Black plays Nxe4, capturing the pawn on e4 and creating hanging pieces on e4 and g4.
White plays Bxe7, capturing the bishop on e7 and creating a hanging queen on a3. A fork is created threatening d8 and f8.
Black plays Qb6, removing the hanging pawn on b7 and attacking b1, b2, b3, b4, and b7.
White played Bc4, attacking the squares d5, e6, and f7. The white bishop on c4 is now hanging.
Black played Nxc3, capturing the white bishop. The black knight on c3 is now hanging.
White played Bc5, attacking the square b6. A fork is created threatening squares b6 and f8.
Black played Rfe8+, giving check. The newly attacked squares are e1, e3, and e7.
White played Kf1, moving the king to f1. The white pawn on g2 is now hanging.
Black played Be6, attacking the squares c4 and g4. The black bishop on g4 is no longer hanging.
White played Bxb6, capturing the black pawn on b7. The white queen on a3 is now hanging.
Black played Bxc4+, capturing the white bishop and giving check. A fork is created threatening squares f1 and a2.
White played Kg1, moving the king to g1. The white rook on h1 is now hanging.
Black played Ne2+, giving check. A fork is created threatening squares g1 and d4.
White played Kf1, moving the king back to f1. The white rook on h1 is now hanging again.
Black played Nxd4+, capturing the white pawn and giving check. A fork is created threatening squares f1 and a2.
White played Kg1, moving the king to g1 again. The white rook on h1 is now hanging.
Black played Ne2+, giving check. The newly attacked squares are a1, b2, c1, c3, f4, g1, and g3.
White played Kf1, moving the king back to f1. The white rook on h1 is now hanging once more.
Black played Nc3+, giving check. A fork is created threatening squares f1 and a2.
White played Kg1, moving the king to g1 again. The white rook on h1 is now hanging again.
Black played axb6, capturing the white pawn on b6. The black pawn on b6 is now hanging.
White played Qb4, moving the queen to b4. The white queen on b4 is now hanging.
Black played Ra4, attacking the white queen on b4. The black rook on e8 is now hanging.
White played Qxb6, capturing the black pawn on b6. The white queen on b6 is now hanging.
Black played Nxd1, capturing the white rook on d1. The black knight on d1 is now hanging.
White played h3, attacking the square g4. The white pawn on h3 is now hanging.
Black played Rxa2, capturing the white pawn on a2. The black bishop on c4 is now hanging.
White played Kh2, moving the king to h2. The white king zone is now safer.
Black played Nxf2, capturing the white pawn on f2. The black knight on f2 is now hanging.
White played Re1, moving the rook to e1. The squares e2, e4, e6, e7, and e8 are now attacked.
Black played Rxe1, capturing the white rook on e1. The black rook on e1 is now hanging.
White played Qd8+, giving check. The white queen moved from b6 to d8, creating new threats.
Black played Bf8, reducing enemy attacks in the black king zone. The black bishop moved from g7 to f8.
White played Nxe1, capturing a piece. The white knight moved from f3 to e1.
Black played Bd5, attacking new squares. The black bishop moved from c4 to d5.
White played Nf3, increasing control around the king zone. The white knight moved from e1 to f3, creating a fork on d5 and f8.
Black played Ne4, changing the square control. The black knight moved from f2 to e4.
White played Qb8, changing the square control. The white queen moved from d8 to b8.
Black played b5, removing a hanging piece. The black pawn moved from b7 to b5.
White played h4, slightly reducing square control. The white pawn moved from h3 to h4.
Black played h5, increasing control in the area. The black pawn moved from h7 to h5.
White played Ne5, changing square control. The white knight moved from f3 to e5.
Black played Kg7, increasing control around the king zone. The black king moved from g8 to g7.
White played Kg1, moving the king to safety. The white king moved from h2 to g1.
Black played Bc5+, giving check. The black bishop moved from f8 to c5.
White played Kf1, moving the king to evade check. The white king moved from g1 to f1.
Black played Ng3+, giving check. The black knight moved from e4 to g3.
White played Ke1, moving the king to evade check. The white king moved from f1 to e1.
Black played Bb4+, giving check. The black bishop moved from c5 to b4.
White played Kd1, moving the king to evade check. The white king moved from e1 to d1.
Black played Bb3+, giving check. The black bishop moved from d5 to b3.
White played Kc1, moving the king to evade check. The white king moved from d1 to c1.
Black played Ne2+, giving check. The black knight moved from g3 to e2.
White played Kb1, moving the king to evade check. The white king moved from c1 to b1.
Black played Nc3+, giving check. The black knight moved from e2 to c3.
White played Kc1, moving the king to evade check. The white king moved from b1 to c1.
Black played Rc2#, delivering checkmate. The black rook moved from a2 to c2.
